items.title: China’s Z.ai claims it can match Mythos on cybersecurity
items.summary: China's Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it matches Mythos in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models from Anthropic and OpenAI in other, more general tasks, it seems that China has dramatically reduced the

items.title: Suno launches Spark incubator program to feed independent artists to its AI machine
items.summary: Suno has ambitions to be more than just a toy to churn out AI slop, it also wants to be a streaming destination and to break new artists. Spark is their new incubator program for independent artists that provides grants, mentorship, and marketing support. To apply, artists need to be an unsigned sin

items.title: Ford rehires ‘gray beard’ engineers after AI falls short
items.summary: "Mistakenly we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence ... that would produce a high-quality product.”

items.title: Why Wall Street thinks US memory maker Micron is the next Nvidia
items.summary: Eager to find more public AI-related companies that may do as well as Nvidia, Wall Street investors think they've found a winner with Micron.

items.title: Prosecutors used ChatGPT logs as evidence in the Palisades fire trial
items.summary: Jonathan Rinderknecht was facing arson charges for setting a fire on New Year's Day in 2025, which became one of the deadliest wildfires in LA history. To make their case, prosecutors turned to location data from his iPhone, security camera footage, and witness testimony. But they also turned to his

items.title: SoftBank’s CEO isn’t the only one with questions about Elon Musk’s orbital data center hype
items.summary: Not everyone is buying Elon Musk’s vision for orbital data centers.

items.title: Margaret Atwood says the problem with AI is ‘garbage in, garbage out’
items.summary: Maraget Atwood, the storied author of The Handmaid's Tale and The Blind Assassin, was interviewed as part of the Babell Literary and Cultural Festival in Porto, Portugal. items.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
items.summary: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

items.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
items.summary: When confronted with cancer, Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

items.title: Why is Apple asking me to pay more for Big Tech’s AI obsession?
items.summary: Tim Cook recently said price increases were "unavoidable" and described the company's pricing as "unsustainable." The 16-inch MacBook Pro saw its price go up by $300. items.title: ConlangCrafter Turns AI to Imagining Languages
items.summary: There are over 7,000 natural languages today, but that doesn’t stop people from occasionally making up completely new ones. items.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like  models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
items.summary: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

items.title: Trump Admin releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
items.summary: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

items.title: Anthropic’s Mythos 5 is back
items.summary: After a rollercoaster negotiation process with the Trump administration that dragged on for two weeks, Anthropic's Mythos 5 is finally back in action - at least, somewhat, for a select group of organizations, according to a letter from the government to Anthropic that was viewed by The Verge. items.title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
items.summary: Half-million strong military will train on drones as “universal combat tool.”

items.summary: NYT shifts OpenAI/Microsoft copyright claims after SCOTUS ruling against Sony.

items.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
items.summary: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

items.title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
items.summary: The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.

items.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.6 amid US AI regulatory drama
items.summary: Less than 24 hours after news broke that OpenAI would stagger its next model release at the request of the Trump administration, that model, GPT-5.6, is here. items.title: It’s not about Anthropic vs. OpenAI anymore
items.summary: AI models have progressed to the point where their capabilities have real political consequences. Dealing with those consequences will require collective action.

items.summary: It's been two weeks since Anthropic took its Mythos-class models offline after a Friday evening ultimatum from the Trump administration. items.title: Heat waves mess with your brain. Scientists are trying to figure out why.

items.title: Run a vLLM Server on HF Jobs in One Command
The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
items.summary: OpenAI reportedly plans to share its newest model, GPT 5.6, with a select group of partners instead of with the broader public. The reason: the Trump administration told it to.
